  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples

GPIO Device API (gxio/gpio.h)
[gxio Low-Level IO Device Control API]

Detailed Description

The GPIO shim allows access to the processor's general purpose I/O pins.

These pins are normally used for low-speed devices like switches and indicators, or to perform board functions such as device reset or interrupt notification. GPIO pins can also be used for moderate-speed data transfer, emulating a low-speed communications bus via software control.

The gxio_gpio API, declared in <gxio/gpio.h>, allows applications to allocate particular GPIO pins, then assert, deassert, or sense the state of those pins. The API is designed to be a minimal wrapper around the GPIO hardware, making system calls only for configuration purposes.

We strongly recommend reading the GPIO section of the IO Device Guide (UG404) before working with this API. Most functions in the gxio_gpio API are directly analogous to hardware interfaces, and the documentation assumes that the reader understands those hardware interfaces. Proper configuration and use of GPIO pins generally requires knowledge of specific details of the system design, such as might be found in the hardware reference manual or board schematics. Note that in many systems, a subset of the available pins are used for board control purposes and are not available via the gxio_gpio API.

Data Structures

struct  gxio_gpio_context_t
 A context object used to manage GPIO hardware resources. More...

Functions

int gxio_gpio_init (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, int gpio_index)
 Initialize a GPIO context.
int gxio_gpio_destroy (gxio_gpio_context_t *context)
 Destroy a GPIO context.
int gxio_gpio_attach (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t pin_mask)
 Request control of a set of GPIO pins.
uint64_t gxio_gpio_get (gxio_gpio_context_t *context)
 Return the current state of a context's attached GPIO pins.
void gxio_gpio_set (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t pins, uint64_t mask)
 Set the output state of some of a context's attached GPIO pins.
void gxio_gpio_toggle (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t pins)
 Toggle (invert) the output state of some of a context's attached GPIO pins.
void gxio_gpio_pulse_assert (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t pins)
 Assert some of a context's attached GPIO output pins for one GPIO clock cycle.
void gxio_gpio_pulse_deassert (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t pins)
 Deassert some of a context's attached GPIO output pins for one GPIO clock cycle.
void gxio_gpio_release (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t pins)
 Release some of a context's attached GPIO output pins.
int gxio_gpio_get_elec (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, unsigned int pin, GPIO_PAD_CONTROL_t *pad_ctl)
 Retrieve the current electrical settings for one of a context's attached GPIO pins.
int gxio_gpio_set_elec (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, unsigned int pin, GPIO_PAD_CONTROL_t pad_ctl)
 Change the current electrical settings for one of a context's attached GPIO pins.
int gxio_gpio_get_gclk_mode (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, GPIO_GCLK_MODE_t *gclk_mode)
 Retrieve the current clock settings for a context's GPIO shim.
int gxio_gpio_set_gclk_mode (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, GPIO_GCLK_MODE_t gclk_mode)
 Change the current clock settings for a context's GPIO shim.
uint64_t gxio_gpio_get_out_inv (gxio_gpio_context_t *context)
 Retrieve the current output inversion settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.
void gxio_gpio_set_out_inv (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t pins)
 Change the current output inversion settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.
uint64_t gxio_gpio_get_in_inv (gxio_gpio_context_t *context)
 Retrieve the current input inversion settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.
void gxio_gpio_set_in_inv (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t pins)
 Change the current input inversion settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.
uint64_t gxio_gpio_get_out_mask (gxio_gpio_context_t *context)
 Retrieve the current output mask settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.
void gxio_gpio_set_out_mask (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t pins)
 Change the current output mask settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.
uint64_t gxio_gpio_get_in_mask (gxio_gpio_context_t *context)
 Retrieve the current input mask settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.
void gxio_gpio_set_in_mask (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t pins)
 Change the current input mask settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.
uint64_t gxio_gpio_get_in_sync (gxio_gpio_context_t *context)
 Retrieve the current input synchronization settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.
void gxio_gpio_set_in_sync (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t pins)
 Change the current input synchronization settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.
uint64_t gxio_gpio_get_in_cnd (gxio_gpio_context_t *context)
 Retrieve the current input sampling settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.
void gxio_gpio_set_in_cnd (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t pins)
 Change the current input sampling settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.
int gxio_gpio_get_dir (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t *disabled_pins, uint64_t *input_pins, uint64_t *output_pins, uint64_t *output_od_pins)
 Retrieve the current input/output settings for a context's GPIO pins.
int gxio_gpio_set_dir (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t disabled_pins, uint64_t input_pins, uint64_t output_pins, uint64_t output_od_pins)
 Modify the current input/output settings for a context's GPIO pins.
int gxio_gpio_cfg_interrupt (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, int target_cpu, int target_event, uint64_t on_assert, uint64_t on_deassert)
 Request GPIO pin interrupts.
void gxio_gpio_report_interrupt (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t *asserted, uint64_t *deasserted)
 Report GPIO pin interrupts.
void gxio_gpio_report_reset_interrupt (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t *asserted, uint64_t *deasserted)
 Report and reenable GPIO pin interrupts.
void gxio_gpio_reset_interrupt (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t en_assert, uint64_t en_deassert)
 Reenable GPIO pin interrupts.
int gxio_gpio_get_pollfd (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, uint64_t on_assert, uint64_t on_deassert)
 Get a file descriptor which may be polled to determine GPIO pin state changes.
int gxio_gpio_arm_pollfd (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, int fd)
 Arm a pollable file descriptor.
int gxio_gpio_get_pinset (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, const char *name, uint64_t *input_pins, uint64_t *output_pins, uint64_t *output_od_pins, uint64_t *inverted_pins)
 Look up a named set of GPIO pins and retrieve pin characteristics.
int gxio_gpio_enumerate_pinset (gxio_gpio_context_t *context, int index, char *name, uint64_t *input_pins, uint64_t *output_pins, uint64_t *output_od_pins, uint64_t *inverted_pins)
 Retrieve the properties of one of this system's named sets of GPIO pins.

Function Documentation

int gxio_gpio_init ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
int  gpio_index 
)

Initialize a GPIO context.

A properly initialized context must be obtained before any of the other gxio_gpio routines may be used.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a gxio_gpio_context_t, which will be initialized by this routine, if it succeeds.
gpio_index Index of the GPIO shim to use.
Returns:
Zero if the context was successfully initialized, else a GXIO_ERR_xxx error code.

int gxio_gpio_destroy ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context  ) 

Destroy a GPIO context.

Once destroyed, a context may not be used with any gxio_gpio routines other than gxio_gpio_init(). After this routine returns, no further interrupts requested on this context will be delivered. The state and configuration of the pins which had been attached to this context are unchanged by this operation.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a gxio_gpio_context_t.
Returns:
Zero if the context was successfully destroyed, else a GXIO_ERR_xxx error code.

int gxio_gpio_attach ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  pin_mask 
)

Request control of a set of GPIO pins.

This routine must be called before any pin state can be sensed or modified. Attempts to set, clear, or sense the state of pins which have not been attached to via this routine will fail silently. Attempts to reconfigure pins which have not been attached to via this routine will return an error.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pin_mask Mask of pins to be associated with this context. If more than one successful call to this routine is made, the set of pins attached will be those specified on the last successful call; in other words, this routine does not add to the set of attached pins, it defines that set.
Returns:
Zero if all of the requested pins were available and were successfully associated with this context. If any of the requested pins are not available for use, either GXIO_GPIO_ERR_PINS_UNAVAIL or GXIO_GPIO_ERR_PINS_BUSY will be returned, and the set of pins attached to this context will remain unchanged.

uint64_t gxio_gpio_get ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context  ) 

Return the current state of a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
Returns:
Mask of pin state. Normally, a pin's bit is 1 if and only if it is an input pin and it is asserted. gxio_gpio_set_input_inv() can invert input pins, and gxio_gpio_set_in_cnd() can cause pins to be sampled even if they are configured as outputs.

void gxio_gpio_set ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  pins,
uint64_t  mask 
)

Set the output state of some of a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pins Mask specifying the new output state for the pins to be modified. By default, a 1 here means that the output signal is asserted; this can be reversed for certain pins with gxio_gpio_set_out_inv().
mask Mask specifying which pins' state is modified; if a pin's bit is off in this mask, its output state is unchanged.

void gxio_gpio_toggle ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  pins 
)

Toggle (invert) the output state of some of a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pins Mask specifying which pins should have their output states toggled.

void gxio_gpio_pulse_assert ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  pins 
)

Assert some of a context's attached GPIO output pins for one GPIO clock cycle.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pins Mask specifying which pins should have their output states temporarily asserted.

void gxio_gpio_pulse_deassert ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  pins 
)

Deassert some of a context's attached GPIO output pins for one GPIO clock cycle.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pins Mask specifying which pins should have their output states temporarily deasserted.

void gxio_gpio_release ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  pins 
)

Release some of a context's attached GPIO output pins.

Any pin whose bit is set in the supplied pin mask will be tri-stated, ignoring the current output state. A subsequent call to this routine which clears that bit in the pin mask will resume that pin's output.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pins Mask specifying which pins should have their output states tri-stated.

int gxio_gpio_get_elec ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  pin,
GPIO_PAD_CONTROL_t *  pad_ctl 
)

Retrieve the current electrical settings for one of a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pin Number of the pin whose settings should be retrieved.
pad_ctl On successful return, current settings for the specified pin.
Returns:
Zero if the settings were retrieved successfully; a nonzero error code if the process had not successfully attached to the relevant pin.

int gxio_gpio_set_elec ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  pin,
GPIO_PAD_CONTROL_t  pad_ctl 
)

Change the current electrical settings for one of a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pin Number of the pin whose settings should be changed.
pad_ctl New settings for the specified pin.
Returns:
Zero if the register was updated successfully; a nonzero error code if the process had not successfully attached to the relevant pin, or if the specific parameters are found to be outside acceptable limits.

int gxio_gpio_get_gclk_mode ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
GPIO_GCLK_MODE_t *  gclk_mode 
)

Retrieve the current clock settings for a context's GPIO shim.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
gclk_mode On successful return, current clock settings.
Returns:
Zero if the settings were retrieved successfully; a nonzero error code otherwise.

int gxio_gpio_set_gclk_mode ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
GPIO_GCLK_MODE_t  gclk_mode 
)

Change the current clock settings for a context's GPIO shim.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
gclk_mode New settings for the shim.
Returns:
Zero if the register was updated successfully; a nonzero error code if the process is not allowed to update this register, or if the specific parameters are found to be outside acceptable limits.

uint64_t gxio_gpio_get_out_inv ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context  ) 

Retrieve the current output inversion settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
Returns:
Mask of current settings for the pins; 1 if a pin's output state is inverted.

void gxio_gpio_set_out_inv ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  pins 
)

Change the current output inversion settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pins Mask of new settings for the pins; 1 if a pin's output state is to be inverted.

uint64_t gxio_gpio_get_in_inv ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context  ) 

Retrieve the current input inversion settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
Returns:
Mask of current settings for the pins; 1 if a pin's input state is inverted.

void gxio_gpio_set_in_inv ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  pins 
)

Change the current input inversion settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pins Mask of new settings for the pins; 1 if a pin's input state is to be inverted.

uint64_t gxio_gpio_get_out_mask ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context  ) 

Retrieve the current output mask settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
Returns:
Mask of current settings for the pins; 1 if a pin's output state is masked. The output state of masked pins is not affected by calls to gxio_gpio_set().

void gxio_gpio_set_out_mask ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  pins 
)

Change the current output mask settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pins Mask of new settings for the pins; 1 if a pin's output state is to be masked. The output state of masked pins is not affected by calls to gxio_gpio_set().

uint64_t gxio_gpio_get_in_mask ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context  ) 

Retrieve the current input mask settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
Returns:
Mask of current settings for the pins; 1 if a pin's input state is masked. The input state of masked pins is not reflected in the result of gxio_gpio_get(); masked pins always return zero.

void gxio_gpio_set_in_mask ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  pins 
)

Change the current input mask settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pins Mask of new settings for the pins; 1 if a pin's input state is to be masked. The input state of masked pins is not reflected in the result of gxio_gpio_get(); masked pins always return zero.

uint64_t gxio_gpio_get_in_sync ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context  ) 

Retrieve the current input synchronization settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
Returns:
Mask of current settings for the pins; 1 if a pin's input state is synchronized.

void gxio_gpio_set_in_sync ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  pins 
)

Change the current input synchronization settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pins Mask of new settings for the pins; 1 if a pin's input state is to be synchronized.

uint64_t gxio_gpio_get_in_cnd ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context  ) 

Retrieve the current input sampling settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
Returns:
Mask of current settings for the pins; 1 if a pin's state state is sampled even when it is configured as an output.

void gxio_gpio_set_in_cnd ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  pins 
)

Change the current input sampling settings for a context's attached GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
pins Mask of new settings for the pins; 1 if a pin's state state is to be sampled even when it is configured as an output.

int gxio_gpio_get_dir ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t *  disabled_pins,
uint64_t *  input_pins,
uint64_t *  output_pins,
uint64_t *  output_od_pins 
)

Retrieve the current input/output settings for a context's GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
disabled_pins Pointer to a pin mask. Upon return, this mask will contain a 1 bit corresponding to each pin which is disabled.
input_pins Pointer to a pin mask. Upon return, this mask will contain a 1 bit corresponding to each pin which is configured as an input pin.
output_pins Pointer to a pin mask. Upon return, this mask will contain a 1 bit corresponding to each pin which is configured as an output pin.
output_od_pins Pointer to a pin mask. Upon return, this mask will contain a 1 bit corresponding to each pin which is configured as an open-drain output pin.

int gxio_gpio_set_dir ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  disabled_pins,
uint64_t  input_pins,
uint64_t  output_pins,
uint64_t  output_od_pins 
)

Modify the current input/output settings for a context's GPIO pins.

The settings for pins not specified in any mask are unchanged. If pins are specified in more than one mask, the behavior is undefined.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
disabled_pins Mask of pins which should be disabled.
input_pins Mask of pins to be configured for input.
output_pins Mask of pins to be configured for output.
output_od_pins Mask of pins to be configured for open-drain output.

int gxio_gpio_cfg_interrupt ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
int  target_cpu,
int  target_event,
uint64_t  on_assert,
uint64_t  on_deassert 
)

Request GPIO pin interrupts.

Request that interrupts be delivered to a tile when input pins attached to a context are asserted and/or deasserted. Once such an interrupt is delivered for a specific pin, that specific interrupt will no longer be delivered for that pin until gxio_gpio_reset_interrupt() is called. The same GPIO pin and assertion/deassertion status may not be concurrently specified in more than one of gxio_gpio_cfg_interrupt() or gxio_gpio_cfgpoll(); attempts to do so will cause undefined results.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
target_cpu CPU number of interrupt target tile.
target_event Specific event which will be set in the IPI0 register when the interrupt occurs. If this event is -1, then the interrupt events specified in on_assert/on_deassert will be disabled for the specified pins.
on_assert Mask of pins whose assertion will cause this interrupt. If more than one successful call to this routine is made, the set of pins which interrupt is the union of those specified on all successful calls.
on_deassert Mask of pins whose deassertion will cause this interrupt. If more than one successful call to this routine is made, the set of pins which interrupt is the union of those specified on all successful calls.
Returns:
Zero if all of the requested pins were successfully configured to interrupt. If any of the requested pins are not attached to this context, GXIO_GPIO_ERR_PINS_UNAVAIL will be returned, and no pins' interrupt state will be changed.

void gxio_gpio_report_interrupt ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t *  asserted,
uint64_t *  deasserted 
)

Report GPIO pin interrupts.

Retrieve the interrupt state of all GPIO pins. This can be useful in determining which pin caused an interrupt when multiple pins are associated with the same target event. Note that the current state of the pin may have changed since the interrupt occurred. Note also that even after this routine has noted that a pin caused an interrupt, until the interrupt status is reset with gxio_gpio_reset_interrupt() or gxio_gpio_report_reset_interrupt(), subsequent calls to this routine will continue to report that pin as having caused an interrupt.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
asserted Pointer to a mask which will be written with the set of pins which have interrupted due to pin assertion and whose interrupt status has not been reset. May be NULL if no information on assertion interrupts is desired.
deasserted Pointer to a mask which will be written with the set of pins which have interrupted due to pin deassertion and whose interrupt status has not been reset. May be NULL if no information on deassertion interrupts is desired.

void gxio_gpio_report_reset_interrupt ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t *  asserted,
uint64_t *  deasserted 
)

Report and reenable GPIO pin interrupts.

Retrieve the interrupt state of all GPIO pins, and reenable interrupts on pins which had previously caused an interrupt. This can be useful in determining which pin caused an interrupt when multiple pins are associated with the same target event. Note that the current state of the pin may have changed since the interrupt occurred.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
asserted Pointer to a mask which will be written with the set of pins which have interrupted due to pin assertion and whose interrupt status has not been reset. May be NULL if no information on assertion interrupts is desired; in this case, no assertion interrupts will be reenabled, even if some have occurred.
deasserted Pointer to a mask which will be written with the set of pins which have interrupted due to pin deassertion and whose interrupt status has not been reset. May be NULL if no information on deassertion interrupts is desired; in this case, no deassertion interrupts will be reenabled, even if some have occurred.

void gxio_gpio_reset_interrupt ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  en_assert,
uint64_t  en_deassert 
)

Reenable GPIO pin interrupts.

Request that interrupts be reenabled for a context's attached GPIO pins. As noted in gxio_gpio_cfg_interrupt(), interrupts are one-time events; once a pin assertion or deassertion has produced an interrupt, subsequent occurrences of the same event will not produce an interrupt until it has been reenabled.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
en_assert Mask of pins for which the assertion interrupt should be enabled.
en_deassert Mask of pins for which the deassertion interrupt should be enabled.

int gxio_gpio_get_pollfd ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
uint64_t  on_assert,
uint64_t  on_deassert 
)

Get a file descriptor which may be polled to determine GPIO pin state changes.

Before the returned file descriptor can be used with poll() or select(), it must first be armed with gxio_gpio_arm_pollfd(). See the description of that routine for the precise behavior of the arming operation. Note that the application must not do any read() or write() operations on the returned file descriptor.

The same GPIO pin and assertion/deassertion status may not be concurrently specified in more than one of gxio_gpio_cfg_interrupt() or gxio_gpio_cfgpoll(); attempts to do so will cause undefined results. Similarly, modification of the interrupt state for pins specified in a call to this routine may also cause undefined results.

If a GPIO context is destroyed, any file descriptor returned from this routine specifying that context is no longer valid. If the file descriptor is no longer needed before destruction of the GPIO context, it may be explicitly closed.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
on_assert Mask of pins whose transition from deasserted to asserted will cause the returned and armed file descriptor to become readable.
on_deassert Mask of pins whose transition from asserted to deasserted will cause the returned and armed file descriptor to become readable.
Returns:
If the call was successful, a non-negative file descriptor; otherwise, a negative error code.

int gxio_gpio_arm_pollfd ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
int  fd 
)

Arm a pollable file descriptor.

Calling this routine clears the interrupt status bits which were specified when gxio_gpio_get_pollfd() was called to retrieve the pollable file descriptor. Once any of those interrupt status bits becomes asserted, the file descriptor passed to this routine will appear to be readable, as determined by the poll() or select() routines.

After that first interrupt assertion has occurred, the file descriptor will remain readable until this routine is called again; in other words, there is no more than one transition of readability, from unreadable to readable, per call to this routine.

Applications may request that an asynchronous signal be sent upon interrupt assertion by using fcntl() with the F_SETOWN command on the pollable file descriptor to set a target process ID or process group, and then using fcntl() with the F_SETFL command to set the O_ASYNC flag on that file descriptor. The requested signal is sent on the first transition of the file descriptor from unreadable to readable. No more than one signal will be sent per call to this routine.

Note that GPIO pins may change while this routine is executing, and that multiple changes may happen simultaneously. Applications waiting for a specific pin state should check for that state after calling this routine, since the state may have been attained before the interrupt status was reset; in that case there would be no readability transition on the file descriptor, and no signal, since GPIO interrupts are edge- and not level-sensitive.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
fd File descriptor obtained from gxio_gpio_get_pollfd().
Returns:
If the call was successful, zero; otherwise, a negative error code.

int gxio_gpio_get_pinset ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
const char *  name,
uint64_t *  input_pins,
uint64_t *  output_pins,
uint64_t *  output_od_pins,
uint64_t *  inverted_pins 
)

Look up a named set of GPIO pins and retrieve pin characteristics.

Pinset names are system-dependent, but are generally hierarchical; for instance, "panel/led/0" might be the pin connected to the first front-panel LED, while "IDE/0/data" might be the pins on the data bus for IDE slot 0. Pins can be configurable in more than one way (for instance, a pin might be usable as either an input or an output) and may be members of more than one pinset.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
name Name to be looked up.
input_pins Pointer to a pin mask. Upon return, this mask will contain a 1 bit corresponding to each pin in the named set which could be used as an input pin.
output_pins Pointer to a pin mask. Upon return, this mask will contain a 1 bit corresponding to each pin in the named set which could be used as an output pin.
output_od_pins Pointer to a pin mask. Upon return, this mask will contain a 1 bit corresponding to each pin in the named set which could be used as an open-drain output pin.
inverted_pins Pointer to a pin mask. Upon return, this mask will contain a 1 bit corresponding to each pin in the named set which should be configured as an inverted pin.
Returns:
If the call was successful, zero; otherwise, a negative error code.

int gxio_gpio_enumerate_pinset ( gxio_gpio_context_t *  context,
int  index,
char *  name,
uint64_t *  input_pins,
uint64_t *  output_pins,
uint64_t *  output_od_pins,
uint64_t *  inverted_pins 
)

Retrieve the properties of one of this system's named sets of GPIO pins.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_gpio_context_t.
index Pinset index. If a system supports N named sets of pins, then indices between 0 and N - 1, inclusive, each correspond to one of those sets. Thus, to retrieve all of a system's named pinsets, call this function in a loop, starting with an index of zero, and incrementing it once per iteration until an error is returned.
name Pointer to the buffer which will receive the name of the set. The buffer should contain space for at least GXIO_GPIO_PIN_NAME_LEN bytes; the returned name, including the terminating null byte, will be no longer than that.
input_pins Pointer to a pin mask. Upon return, this mask will contain a 1 bit corresponding to each pin in the named set which could be used as an input pin.
output_pins Pointer to a pin mask. Upon return, this mask will contain a 1 bit corresponding to each pin in the named set which could be used as an output pin.
output_od_pins Pointer to a pin mask. Upon return, this mask will contain a 1 bit corresponding to each pin in the named set which could be used as an open-drain output pin.
inverted_pins Pointer to a pin mask. Upon return, this mask will contain a 1 bit corresponding to each pin in the named set which should be configured as an inverted pin.
Returns:
Zero if a named set of pins was successfully retrieved; a negative error code if one was not.
